content: Just as the linguistic definition of reflexion frames it as both a passive physical process of light bouncing back (reflection) and an active deliberate cognitive practice of反思 (self-examination) in human learning, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be split into two under-explored complementary modes: passive residual reflexion that retraces existing inference paths to surface hidden inconsistencies, and active iterative reflexion that explicitly revises reasoning like human deliberate self-examination. This split offers a new framework to design more robust LLM self-reflection systems.
